blurb: Manages the province's finances — budget, taxation, public sector employer policy — and regulates financial services, real estate, and gambling policy.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians and businesses can pay provincial taxes easily and accurately
goals.description: The ministry administers B.C.'s major taxes — provincial sales tax, income tax, and property transfer tax — and provides eTaxBC as a single online service for filing, paying, and managing tax accounts.

main_services.name: Register and pay provincial sales tax

supporting.name: Register to collect PST
supporting.desc: Businesses that sell or lease taxable goods, software, or services in B.C. must register to collect and remit provincial sales tax (PST); some out-of-province and voluntary registrants may also register.

legislation.full_name: Provincial Sales Tax Act  -  Registration to collect tax

relationships.service: Register a sole proprietorship or partnership

supporting.name: Register to collect tax on short-term accommodation
supporting.desc: Businesses that sell accommodation in B.C., such as hotels and short-term rentals, must register to collect PST and, where it applies, the Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT) under the same registration.

channels.label: Register to collect PST — accommodation providers

legislation.full_name: Provincial Sales Tax Act  -  Accommodation and MRDT registration

supporting.name: File and pay your provincial taxes through eTaxBC
supporting.desc: eTaxBC is a free, secure online service for individuals and businesses to file returns, make payments, and communicate with the Ministry of Finance about PST and other provincial taxes and fees.

supporting.name: Claim a personal income tax credit
supporting.desc: B.C. offers refundable and non-refundable personal income tax credits, such as the renter's tax credit, training credit, and climate action credit, claimed on the annual income tax return.

legislation.full_name: Income Tax Act  -  Personal tax credits

supporting.name: Claim a corporate income tax credit
supporting.desc: Corporations may qualify for tax credits such as the scientific research and experimental development, film and television, interactive digital media, or manufacturing and processing investment credits.

legislation.full_name: Income Tax Act  -  Corporate tax credits

supporting.name: Pay property transfer tax when you buy property
supporting.desc: Anyone who purchases or gains a registered interest in B.C. property must file a property transfer tax return and pay tax based on the property's fair market value, unless an exemption applies.

legislation.full_name: Property Transfer Tax Act  -  Tax on registration of property transfers

supporting.name: Claim a property transfer tax exemption
supporting.desc: Buyers may qualify to reduce or eliminate property transfer tax through programs such as the first time home buyers' program, the newly built home exemption, or family and farm transfer exemptions.
supporting.users: First-time home buyers, families, farm owners

legislation.full_name: Property Transfer Tax Act  -  Exemptions

supporting.name: Pay the additional property transfer tax as a foreign buyer
supporting.desc: Foreign nationals, foreign corporations, and taxable trustees must pay an additional property transfer tax on the residential portion of property purchased within specified areas of B.C.

legislation.full_name: Property Transfer Tax Act  -  Additional tax for foreign entities

supporting.name: File a BC home flipping tax return
supporting.desc: Individuals, corporations, partnerships, or trusts that dispose of a taxable residential property owned for less than 730 days must file a BC home flipping tax return and may owe tax on the profit.

channels.label: File your BC home flipping tax return

legislation.full_name: Residential Property (Short-Term Holding) Profit Tax Act  -  Tax on profit from short-term property sales

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Property owners get fair assessments and the tax relief they qualify for
goals.description: The ministry sets policy for annual property tax relief programs like the home owner grant and tax deferment, oversees the independent property assessment appeal process, and administers the speculation and vacancy tax to help address the housing crisis.

main_services.name: Reduce your annual property tax bill

supporting.name: Claim the home owner grant
supporting.desc: The home owner grant reduces the property taxes homeowners pay each year on their principal residence, with a higher additional grant available to seniors, veterans, and people with disabilities.
supporting.users: Homeowners, seniors, veterans, people with disabilities

legislation.full_name: Home Owner Grant Act  -  Home owner grant

supporting.name: Defer your property taxes
supporting.desc: The property tax deferment program is a low-interest loan program that lets eligible homeowners — including those 55 or older, surviving spouses, people with disabilities, and families with children — defer their current year property taxes.
supporting.users: Homeowners 55+, families with children, people with disabilities

supporting.name: Appeal your property assessment
supporting.desc: Property owners who disagree with their BC Assessment property assessment, classification, or exemptions can file a complaint with BC Assessment by January 31 for a hearing before an independently appointed Property Assessment Review Panel.

main_services.name: Comply with the speculation and vacancy tax

supporting.name: Check if your property is subject to the speculation and vacancy tax
supporting.desc: Owners of residential property can use an interactive map to check whether their property is within a designated taxable area for the speculation and vacancy tax.

legislation.full_name: Speculation and Vacancy Tax Act  -  Designated taxable areas

supporting.name: File a speculation and vacancy tax declaration
supporting.desc: Residential property owners in designated taxable areas — including individuals, corporations, trusts, and business partnerships — must declare every year for the speculation and vacancy tax by March 31, even with no change to their information.

legislation.full_name: Speculation and Vacancy Tax Act  -  Annual declaration requirement

supporting.name: Claim a speculation and vacancy tax exemption or credit
supporting.desc: Property owners in taxable areas may qualify for an exemption from the speculation and vacancy tax, or for a tax credit that reduces the amount owed if no exemption applies.

legislation.full_name: Speculation and Vacancy Tax Act  -  Exemptions and tax credits

supporting.name: Pay the speculation and vacancy tax
supporting.desc: Owners who owe speculation and vacancy tax pay by the first business day in July, based on the Notice of Assessment mailed after their declaration or an audit.

legislation.full_name: Speculation and Vacancy Tax Act  -  Payment of tax

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Financial services and the public sector operate with sound oversight and transparency
goals.description: The ministry oversees financial sector regulation through the BC Financial Services Authority, sets compensation and governance policy for the broader public sector, and administers pay transparency reporting requirements for employers.

main_services.name: Access regulated financial services with confidence

supporting.name: Get information about a regulated mortgage broker, real estate professional, or financial institution
supporting.desc: The BC Financial Services Authority (BCFSA), a Crown agency accountable to the Minister of Finance, supervises and regulates credit unions, insurance, mortgage brokers, pensions, real estate professionals, and trusts.

legislation.full_name: Financial Institutions Act  -  Regulation of financial services sector

supporting.name: Report a concern about a regulated financial services provider
supporting.desc: Members of the public can report a concern about a credit union, insurer, mortgage broker, pension plan, real estate professional, or trust company to the BC Financial Services Authority for investigation.

legislation.full_name: Financial Institutions Act  -  Public protection and complaints

main_services.name: Oversee public sector compensation and governance

supporting.name: Access public sector executive compensation disclosures
supporting.desc: Crown corporations, health authorities, post-secondary institutions, school districts, and the BC Public Service must publicly disclose compensation paid to their CEO and next four highest-paid executives each year.

legislation.full_name: Public Sector Employers Act  -  Executive compensation disclosure

supporting.name: Apply to serve on a public sector board or agency
supporting.desc: The Crown Agencies and Board Resourcing Office recruits and recommends candidates for appointment to B.C.'s Crown corporations, agencies, boards, and commissions, and provides governance training for appointees.

channels.label: Crown Agencies and Board Resourcing Office
Learn about & apply for public boards

supporting.name: Publish a pay transparency report as an employer
supporting.desc: Reporting employers — the B.C. government, certain Crown corporations, and businesses over a phased employee-count threshold — must prepare and publicly post an annual pay transparency report by November 1 each year under the Pay Transparency Act.

legislation.full_name: Pay Transparency Act  -  Employer reporting obligations

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Government's budget and finances are transparent and well managed
goals.description: The ministry prepares the provincial Budget, publishes the Public Accounts and other financial reports, and provides billing and payment services for amounts owed to government.

main_services.name: Track the provincial budget and financial results

supporting.name: Review the B.C. budget
supporting.desc: The BC Budget website provides a reader-friendly, plain-language overview of the provincial budget, including tax changes announced each year.

legislation.full_name: Budget Transparency and Accountability Act  -  Budget documents

supporting.name: Access Public Accounts and government financial statements
supporting.desc: The Public Accounts include the Province's audited annual financial statements and other key financial information, published under the Budget Transparency and Accountability Act, along with the Ministerial Accountability Report comparing expected and actual results.

legislation.full_name: Budget Transparency and Accountability Act  -  Public Accounts
Balanced Budget and Ministerial Accountability Act  -  Ministerial Accountability Report

supporting.name: Pay and manage your Revenue Services of BC account
supporting.desc: Revenue Services of BC's Billing & Payment Services lets people track, pay, and manage bills for ambulance services, BC student loans, court fines, employment and assistance overpayments, the Medical Services Plan, and other government accounts in one profile.

foundational_services.name: Tax policy development and revenue forecasting
foundational_services.desc: Establishes, implements, and reviews the province's economic, fiscal, financial management, and taxation policies, and forecasts government revenue to inform the annual budget.

foundational_services.name: Financial reporting, accounting standards, and internal audit for government
foundational_services.desc: The Office of the Comptroller General sets accounting policy and standards, prepares the Public Accounts, and provides internal audit and financial reporting oversight across ministries and the broader public sector.

legislation.full_name: Budget Transparency and Accountability Act  -  Financial reporting requirements
